title: Would a psychologist really have helped the situation
display_title: Would a psychologist really have helped the situation
wikitext:
[]: Would a psychologist really have helped the situation?             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Thu Apr 18, 2013 11:39 am I hear a lot of people say that the main cast would've been much better off if they'd gone to therapy. After all, what kind of organization doesn't use it for people who clearly need it? I used to agree with them until I realized that therapy may not have helped at all. Especially for people like Gendo or Ritsuko who've pretty much come to accept their madness as part of themselves. Which pretty much means that the world is still screwed. It may temporarily help characters like Shinji or Misato, but to what end? Anyone else think that therapy would've only delayed the inevitable or could it have made an impact on the direction of the series?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Apr 18, 2013 1:52 pm Given that the dénouement is controlled by superhuman forces, little details like keeping both Shinji and Asuka functional to the end would have altered very little. It might have forced Yui to show her hand a little sooner, if it looked like Asuka was about to take the harpies down properly, unless she can figure out on the fly a different way to play the end-game to her own advantage.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Thu Apr 18, 2013 2:22 pm In short-term yeah probably.Long term?It is difficult to say.I always thought why there arent psychologists in NERV for the pilots since most organizations have psychologists for their staff.

[]: Re: Would a psychologist really have helped the situation?             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Apr 18, 2013 3:52 pm LordThaeon wrote: Anyone else think that therapy would've only delayed the inevitable or could it have made an impact on the direction of the series?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Apr 18, 2013 4:41 pm Dima wrote: In short-term yeah probably.Long term?It is difficult to say.I always thought why there arent psychologists in NERV for the pilots since most organizations have psychologists for their staff.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Apr 18, 2013 4:56 pm Of course a psychologist would have made some difference. It's just difficult to predict the exact changes that would have come about from introducing some kind of therapy to the characters.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Apr 18, 2013 5:00 pm At the very least, Shinji would've known he wasn't alone. And arguably, that's all he would've needed.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Apr 18, 2013 5:02 pm AuraTwilight wrote: At the very least, Shinji would've known he wasn't alone. And arguably, that's all he would've needed.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Apr 18, 2013 5:43 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: It would have ended the series right then and there, though. Isn't the whole show just using the Evas and Angels as a medium to tell the story?

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Thu Apr 18, 2013 7:32 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Cue Bob the Angry Flower...

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Apr 18, 2013 7:34 pm Rei IV wrote: That succinctly describes Evangelion in a nutshell. And yes, I honestly believe therapy would've done wonders for the cast, especially Rei and Ritsuko.

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Thu Apr 18, 2013 8:10 pm Asuka seems like a therapy is for the weak kind of person. However, she had training as a child. She never really experienced hardship when it came the the angels like Shinji did in episode 3. Rei would only do it if Gendo wanted her to. Which would never happen. Rei doesn't seem like the kind of person to ask for help. Shinji is a different story. He does ask for it. Expectantly in the later episodes. I think it would be totally irresponsible and incompetent to NOT give him therapy after killing Kaworu. I don't think even a badass marine would be totally normal after doing that. However, I do think it might help Shinji a lot better but then we wouldn't have a show. I think its like one of those questions like "Why don't they just eat Gilligan?" or "Why doesn't Rita kill the Power Rangers when they least expect it?" I think the reason is because of fear of them piloting Eva. Its like they don't want to risk therapy and have their synch ratio go down. That would make a lot of sense but there plenty of things contradicting it. Thou that just a theory. I do believe if this happened in real life, Shinji would have been forced to go to therapy(at least in America). Kendrix wrote: Half the world died, and everyone else suffered the trauma of surviving an apocalypse. The standarts for "sane" are probably not what they once used to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Apr 19, 2013 6:57 am Kendrix wrote: Half the world died, and everyone else suffered the trauma of surviving an apocalypse. The standarts for "sane" are probably not what they once used to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 19, 2013 7:44 am AuraTwilight wrote: At the very least, Shinji would've known he wasn't alone. And arguably, that's all he would've needed.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Fri Apr 19, 2013 7:51 am Psychologists would want to keep the kiddies out of the fucking robots. And Nerv need the kiddies in the fucking robots... One could probably see an explanation there as to why there was no "therapy" in the series proper then.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Apr 19, 2013 7:56 am Blue Monday wrote: Psychologists would want to keep the kiddies out of the fucking robots. And Nerv need the kiddies in the fucking robots... One could probably see an explanation there as to why there was no "therapy" in the series proper then.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Fri Apr 19, 2013 8:08 am Except the police and military aren't made up of emotionally frail 14 year old children, and not to mention all the non-regular psychological variables that are involved in piloting Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Fri Apr 19, 2013 9:10 am I think not even therapy would get Asuka to spill the beans on her past given how well she perfected the confident façade unless they used hypnotherapy or something. Shinji would probably be more open to discussing his problems as long as he's under the impression that therapy was much like any order given to him by NERV since he can't usually think for himself and depends on others to do the thinking for him.

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Tue Apr 23, 2013 3:46 pm No, a psychologist would not have helped. Psychologists in general are useless douches who do slightly less to help you with your problems than an average bartender, but charge as much as high-class escorts. Shinji would have been helped much more by just getting him a hooker, or at least finding new excuses to send him to Rei's apartment while she's naked. What if that was Ritsuko's and/or Misato's plan all along? What if they were like "damn, these kids need to get laid. Maybe we should send him over to her apartment while she's taking a shower and hope for the best?" Lawl, silly Misato Asuka is probably beyond help. Blue Monday wrote: Psychologists would want to keep the kiddies out of the fucking robots. And Nerv need the kiddies in the fucking robots... One could probably see an explanation there as to why there was no "therapy" in the series proper then.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Tue Apr 23, 2013 4:37 pm Elect G-Max wrote: No, a psychologist would not have helped. Psychologists in general are useless douches who do slightly less to help you with your problems than an average bartender, but charge as much as high-class escorts.
